master_row.title: Relation: ⟨ ε_encode Dashv δ_decode ⟩
master_row.canonical_statement: ven by the composition: \begin{equation} \Phi_{\text{total}} = \psi_{\text{quantize}} \circ \pi_{\text{propagate}} \circ \eta_{\text{holographic}} \circ \varepsilon_{\text{encode}} \end{equation} This composition defines a functor from the category of matrix spaces to the category of quantum observables. \end{proposition} \subsection{Adjoint Functors and Natural Transformations} The encoding and decoding operations form an adjoint pair, establishing a fundamental relationship between the blob manifold and the DAANSphere. \begin{theorem}[Encoding-Decoding Adjunction] There exists an adjunction $\langle \varepsilon_{\text{encode}} \dashv \delta_{\text{decode}} \rangle$ with unit $\eta: \text{Id}_{\text{Blob}} \Rightarrow \delta_{\text{decode}} \circ \varepsilon_{\text{encode}}$ and counit $\epsilon: \varepsilon_{\text{encode}} \circ \delta_{\text{decode}} \Rightarrow \text{Id}_{\text{DAAN}}$ satisfying the triangle identities: \begin{align} (\epsilon \circ \varepsilon_{\text{encode}}) \circ (\varepsilon_{\text{encode}} \circ \eta) &= \text{Id}_{\varepsilon_{\text{encode}}}\\ (\delta_{\text{decode}} \circ \epsilon) \circ (\eta \circ \delta_{\text{decode}}) &=

It is computed as an expectation value or trace over a quantum state, producing an observable. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
